memory_recovered: Continuity state can be derived without curated path lists by enumerating every JSON blob under operations/queue-updates from a non-truncated recursive repository tree, fetching every enumerated document, filtering by owner, and applying a deterministic order.

observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is an older canonical snapshot
append-only continuity updates exist after that snapshot
the prior current-view manifest explicitly left completeness unresolved
inferred: a non-truncated recursive tree plus successful fetch and parse of every enumerated update path is sufficient to prove discovery completeness for that tree snapshot
proposed: bind future materialized views to an immutable recursive tree SHA and store the generated current view as an append-only artifact
superseded: manual curated replay lists as the preferred discovery mechanism
unresolved: the GitHub connector used in this run did not expose a recursive tree listing, so repository-wide completeness was tested as a contract but not executed against the live repository tree

verification.method: Node.js built-in test runner against byte-identical committed module and test contents in an isolated runtime
assertions: all enumerated files must be fetched and parsed before completeness is true
other teams' updates are excluded from continuity-owned state
duplicate update identities and malformed JSON fail closed
verification.limit: This verifies reducer behavior, not completeness of the present live repository tree.

falsification_route: Provide a non-truncated recursive tree where an operations/queue-updates JSON blob is omitted by the enumerator, demonstrate that an enumerated path can fail to fetch or parse while completeness_verified remains true, or show nondeterministic output for identical inputs.
